title: When how did you first find out about Eva
display_title: When how did you first find out about Eva
wikitext:
[]: When/how did you first find out about Eva?             Quote Post by Dartz » Sun Jan 11, 2009 10:15 pm Seriously, when? I'm pretty sure I first stumbled across animé in general myself in late 2004, maybe early 2005, when I was in first year at college. I didn't even have The Internet at home at the time. At that stage I was only just beginning to find out about animé and Neon Genesis Evangelion, so I was getting a bit google happy. In fact, I first heard about animé when I ran into the following website: Link And from there, I found out about Neon Genesis Evangelion: Link 2 clips downloaded in December 2004 that got me hooked. That was around the time we first got diallup here. A video that took me about 2 hours to download, will be done in under a minute now. The image of Rei charging forward, in Unit 00, smashing a bomb one handed into a mysterious shield protected enemy, it was compelling. I wanted to know more. The battle with Unit 03 would have to wait, the file was much too large. So I hit google. Evamade, Evafics Evamonkey. Mr Tines' Eva website, The Evangelion article on wikipedia (Which was about 2 pages at the time) Eva-R FFN Any source of information was hoovered up. I remember there being a post on Evamonkey about Rei and Asuka MP3 players. I didn't join any of the sites though. I'm not sure why. Maybe it was a bit of noobish cold feet. the net result of this though, was that I knew the story before I'd seen the series. Hell, I started reading Eva fanfiction out of a desire to know more about this mysterious thing. One day, in my local HMV, I found 'The End of Evangelion'. I didnt care that it was meant to be the last, or that I was technically spoilering the lot for myself... I was *watching* Eva, at home. My first impression was that it was cheaper than Akira, but I didn't care. It was Eva and it was mine. It gave me headaches, and I really had no idea who the fuck everyone was, but it didn't matter. I had seen some of it. And then, the Animé society at my university started up, and I met someone with a fansubbed copy of the series... missing episodes 4 and 5. I swallowed it with joy, ripping through all 24 episodes (minus 4,5) in half a day, daisy chaining them together on a brand new laptop. That was about February 2006. It was also the time I first dipped my toes into the fanfiction water. It was no coincidence that I ended up repeating 2nd year. I was familiar with the name Evamade.org... I saw it mentioned again in a fanfiction on FFN, and I thought I might take a look. Evamade.Org didn't work. Evamade.net did. I looked at the site for a few days, wondering what it might be, mayhap, before eventually plucking up the courage to wander in a register. This was March 2006. I joined EFO in November, and Darkscribes about 6 months after that. Then a shitload of other sites. For such a major site, I found evageeks and That Board last, eventually staggering through the door on whenever the joined date says I did... Most of this was done on the same 1999 vintage PC, Pentium 2, 128Mb of RAM, Windows 98. No Antivirus. It was getting a bit hairy. My own 256Mb RAM laptop in 2006 and the college's wireless network was a revalation in speed compared to that junker, especially since. I didn't even get broadband at home until about a year and a bit ago. I still had to use that bloody dinosaur if I wanted web access s'abhaile. Anyway, I finally finished watching the entire series on October 24th, 2008 when I bought the Platinum tinned set at London Expo, came back to the fleapit hotel, and finally saw Episode's 4 and 5; 4 years after I first heard the name.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Jan 11, 2009 10:23 pm I think I've seen this thread before.... Alas... My EvaPedia Entry wrote: Believe it or not this can be lead back to my dad! At that time I was young, I didn't even have a favorite band! But my dad brought home Weird Al's Poodle Hat CD. I listened to one song, AMAZING!! He was so funny and awesome I listened to the whole CD at least 10 times. Of course we burned the CD and I continued my search for music from my new favorite artist. I listen to a lot of his song's and my favorite was I Think I'm A Clone Now... ...Well after years of research and the unnatural need to do it, I now know a LOT more [about Evangelion] than I did and still like to look into things today. I like to apply most of the psychological aspects to my life and I think that in the end that makes me a better person because of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Deathmask » Sun Jan 11, 2009 10:48 pm Went to the library in 3rd grade and picked up anything that looked interesting. Evangelion was one of them.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sun Jan 11, 2009 10:54 pm We can't all be that lucky.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Jan 11, 2009 11:57 pm Freshman year. This nerdy guy was painstakingly putting the shine and dirt detail on Eva Sho, Ni, and Zerogouki when I asked him what he was drawing, and if it was related to Gundam (I didn't watch Gundam). He nearly bitchslapped me. so I looked it up and torrented the movies. I kept on looking for answers for my questions and found EMF. This guy wrote a kickass essay on why he supports Rei (maybe he was an R/S or something). After a month of lurking I joined, and this is where I am now.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 12, 2009 12:15 am Spring 1999, just after I had turned 16. One of my friends in high school gave me Black Lion on VHS for my birthday. On the tape was an action packed preview for Evangelion, the one with "Ode to Joy". I was look "Cool! Violence!". Has it really been almost 10 years?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Jan 12, 2009 12:19 am Whenever it started playing on AdultSwim, sitting at my house. I saw the first episode and was hooked.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Mon Jan 12, 2009 12:23 am ^ Seconded, 'cept in my case I saw episode 19 at 1 in the morning. At the time I had a very unhealthy strenuous Dance Dance Revolution routine that in short ended up with me getting done around 12:30am. So, DDR indirectly exposed me to Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Jan 12, 2009 12:27 am it was by accident almost three years ago, I was channel surfing late on a Saturday night and I was about to go to bed and I stumbled on Eva when it was on Adult Swim here in the States it was the 26th episode and needless to say I was intrigued so happy I found it because I was going through some serious sh*t that wasn't apocalyptic like the show but was bad enough (look at my very first few posts bn this forum way back in '07 or PM me if interested) EvaMonkey was among the first sites I found that was centered on Eva, I think Tines' was the very first

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Jan 12, 2009 1:09 am Sometime during the early 2000's I'd guess. I saw it first at my younger cousin's house. I think I saw the third episode. At the time I thought it wasn't nearly as cool as this dvd of gundam 08th ms team i had found and watched. But for some reason Evangelion stuck with me. Somehow. In late 2005 when I was a freshman at college, I discovered how torrents worked, and the first thing I downloaded was the entire NGE series (again I have no idea why i even remembered NGE at this point). I left the torrent running in my dorm room over Thanksgiving break. When I got back I started watching it. I ended up marathoning the entire series in one sitting. At the time, I didn't even know EoE existed, but I saw there were OA versions and Directors cut versions of 21-24. To this day, I don't think I've ever seen the OA versions of those (once I went back, but the performances of the Dub VA's was noticably different, and I didn't watch all the way through). I got through 25 and 26 and although confused, I realized even then I had seen something special that would stick with me for a while. It wasn't until the spring semester of 2006 that I found a DVD of EoE in some girl's dorm room. I borrowed it and never gave it back. After watching NGE, I moved onto Trigun, FMA, Ghost in the Shell, Samurai Champloo. Adult Swim was showing these (well dubbed) anime quite a bit at night at the time. A friend down the hall also got me into Naruto, although at the same time, Naruto was the first series I would ever watch subtitled. In the fall of 2006 I was roving the internet and stumbled across some NGE fanfiction, and somehow that in turn led to me finding evamonkey.com

[]:             Quote Post by Hellfire » Mon Jan 12, 2009 1:35 am The first time I ever saw anything related to it: Shinji, Rei, and Asuka on a poster for a show in the late 90's. The first time I actually saw parts of it: an AMV to "It's a Sin" by the Pet Shop Boys in like 2002 or 2003. I thought, "Why do these giant robots have mouths and teeth, and how come they can be choked out? And what's this kid freaking out about? This show looks weird..." The First time I saw an episode: It's Adult Swim run back in like 05 or 06. I didn't see the first episode; I just saw an episode here or there, so I didn't really know what was going on. I thought I didn't really like most the characters; Shinji was annoyingly self-defeating and freaked out about everything, Asuka was a little brat, Rei was quiet and weird, Ritsuko seemed to have a stick up her ass. Misato I thought was pretty cool, but she also seemed to freak out unnecessarily at times. I saw some of the later episodes and got intrigued, but then I saw either one of the last two episodes and thought, "...what the fuck ?" So basically I didn't think I liked it as a whole until last year, when Annihilationscape convinced me to give it another go. And now I think it's awesome. Anyhoo...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 12, 2009 2:36 am I saw the first manga chapter in its first US serialization, long, long ago, in Forbidden Planet, skimmed it and was not terribly impressed. It didn't look as good as Masmune Shirow's work. Fast forward most of a decade, to late 2003, well into the era where DVD drives offer a TV-free media playing solution, and I've started to watch some girls and guns anime titles out on DVD here. And then I spot the first few NGE DVDs, and vaguely recall that the series had a reputation as one of the better giant robot series, and thought, well, what the heck, let's give this a spin. And thereby hangs a tale.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Mon Jan 12, 2009 4:27 am Summer 1999. My friend had the series on VHS and we marathoned it. A month or so later we got our hands on a VHS fansub of EoE. It was the only anime other than DBZ and Akira I'd seen. I think it resonated with me because I was 14 at the time and I thought "Oh wow, I can totally relate. This is so fucking DEEP" &c.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jan 12, 2009 9:42 am 2002. I had just bought the Cowboy Bebop DVD boxset from Amazon, and they sent me one of those annoying, "If you liked that, you'll also enjoy this..." emails. The also enjoy turns out to have been Evangelion. (Un)Lucky for all of you that I clicked on the link.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Jan 12, 2009 3:59 pm In 2002 or so a consultant worked with us on some softwarez; her brother had worked on one of the NGE PC media CDs (screensavers, wallpaper, text stuff), and she gave me a copy. That made me curious enough to watch the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 12, 2009 4:36 pm Well I saw pictures of the characters in magazines like Animerica during the 2000s (for instance, usually Rei or sometimes Asuka or Shinji), but I believe when I first really saw it was in my Webmasters Guild....10th grade (2002, lol), I think it was. Some other members were watching clips on a computer, and one of the scenes was Eva-02/Asuka fighting the JSSDF from Air. The kid who's CD it was said something about how "Asuka kicks serious ass" or "she could have kicked the Eva series ass". I think I asked what show it was (Or probably said something like "Is that Evangelion?" since I've heard the name--Eva is like THE anime after all--just never bothered watching it then*) and then sometime later (like a few months) I borrowed a tape from a friend and watched only episode 05 of the dub (the tape included episodes 05 and 06), but I just couldn't get into it then. (Watching episode 03 on Adult Swim a few years later changed my mind about the show, no idea why.) *I held off on watching it since I heard something about Anno being a major prick and although I was intrigued by it's supposed psychological aspect/character interaction, the fact it was mecha/shoujo scared me off. And also, I just couldn't get into episode 05 dubbed, probably because I had no idea WTF was going on plot-wise.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Mon Jan 12, 2009 6:43 pm A friend showed me something cool in 2002. It was called The End of Evangelion. I was like "alright, wtf happened?" I watched the rest of the series sometime afterwards.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 12, 2009 7:02 pm Zap: Tell us how you really found out about Eva, I'm curious. Kinda amusing so many of us found out in the 2000s. Anybody here find out about the show when it first aired in Japan? (I thought we had a few members that did discover it that way.)

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Mon Jan 12, 2009 7:45 pm That is how I found out about it, you jackass.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 12, 2009 7:54 pm It's hard to tell when you're serious and when you're joking in threads. Sorry.

At that stage I was only just beginning to find out about animé and Neon Genesis Evangelion, so I was getting a bit google hap
